  "decomposition_rationale": "The root node (Student_Grade_System) receives a raw JSON input containing a command and grade_data. It directly decomposes into 10 handler children, each responsible for a specific command. The root itself performs command parsing and routing (implicitly). This design keeps the tree flat (depth 1) and each handler has a focused responsibility. Handlers that are atomic (add_student, add_course, delete_grade, update_grade) stop decomposition. The remaining handlers (record_grade, get_student_grades, get_course_grades, list_class_grades, get_grade_report, get_course_stats) require further decomposition because they involve multiple data sources or complex computations (validations, statistics, report generation). Those handlers will be further decomposed in subsequent steps. All children receive their inputs from the parent's parsed input fields. The parent's global variables (students, courses, grades) are distributed as read/write subsets to children based on their actual data operations. Child functions do not call each other; the root explicitly invokes the correct child based on the command.",

  },
